home.subtitle: A community of hope, rehabilitation, and sustainable living founded by Baba Amte
home.mission_text: Anandwan is dedicated to empowering marginalized communities, especially those affected by leprosy, through healthcare, education, and dignified livelihood opportunities.
home.healthcare_desc: Providing medical care and rehabilitation services for leprosy patients and people with disabilities.
home.education_desc: Running schools and vocational training centers for children and adults from marginalized communities.
home.agriculture_desc: Developing innovative farming practices and promoting food security through organic methods.
home.empowerment_desc: Building self-reliant communities through skill development and income generation activities.
home.testimonial_1: "Coming to Anandwan gave me dignity and purpose. Here, I am not defined by my disability but by my skills and contributions."
home.get_involved_subtitle: Join us in our mission to build an inclusive and self-reliant community.
home.donate_desc: Support our initiatives with your contribution.
home.volunteer_desc: Share your skills and time with our community.
home.spread_awareness_desc: Help us fight stigma and misinformation.

about.title: About Anandwan
about.history_p1: Anandwan was founded in 1949 by social activist and humanitarian Murlidhar Devidas Amte, affectionately known as Baba Amte. What began as a small rehabilitation center for those affected by leprosy has grown into a thriving community and a model for sustainable development.
about.history_p2: With the vision "Charity destroys, work builds," Baba Amte created a self-sufficient community where people were empowered through dignity of labor and self-reliance rather than mere charity.
about.history_p3: Today, Anandwan stands as a testament to what marginalized communities can achieve when given opportunities, respect, and the right support systems.
about.mission_text: To create an inclusive community where individuals marginalized by society due to disabilities, illness, or socioeconomic factors find dignity, purpose, and opportunities for self-reliance through constructive work.
about.vision_text: A world where every individual, regardless of their physical or social condition, has access to healthcare, education, and opportunities to contribute meaningfully to society, leading to self-sufficient and sustainable communities.
about.founder_p1: Murlidhar Devidas Amte, known as Baba Amte, was a noted social worker and social activist known particularly for his work for the rehabilitation and empowerment of people suffering from leprosy.
about.founder_p2: He dedicated his life to serving the marginalized and believed in the philosophy that compassion alone is not enough; it must be accompanied by action. His work earned him numerous accolades including the Ramon Magsaysay Award, the Templeton Prize, and the Gandhi Peace Prize.
about.founder_p3: His legacy lives on through his sons, Vikas and Prakash Amte, who continue to lead the work at Anandwan and its satellite projects.
about.work_healthcare_3: Eye hospital providing free cataract surgeries
about.work_healthcare_5: Mobile medical units for remote villages
about.work_education_1: Schools for children with special needs

awareness.hero_subtitle: Awareness, Facts, and Anandwan's Role in Treatment & Rehabilitation
awareness.what_is_p1: Leprosy, also known as Hansen's disease, is a chronic infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium leprae. It primarily affects the skin, peripheral nerves, mucosa of the upper respiratory tract, and eyes.
awareness.what_is_p2: Contrary to common myths, leprosy is not highly contagious. It spreads through droplets from the nose and mouth during close and frequent contact with untreated cases. With early diagnosis and modern treatment, leprosy is completely curable.
awareness.myth_2: Leprosy causes body parts to fall off.
awareness.myth_3: Leprosy is a curse or punishment for sins.
awareness.fact_1: Leprosy is one of the least contagious infectious diseases.
awareness.fact_2: Disabilities happen due to nerve damage, not the disease itself.
awareness.fact_3: Leprosy is caused by bacteria, not sins or curses.
awareness.fact_4: Leprosy is completely curable with Multi-Drug Therapy (MDT).
awareness.cta_subtitle: Spread awareness, support those affected, and help eliminate the stigma around leprosy.

fundraiser.hero_subtitle: Help us empower marginalized communities with your generous contribution
fundraiser.rehab_center_desc: Your donation will help us construct a state-of-the-art rehabilitation center to provide healthcare, vocational training, and dignified living for individuals affected by leprosy and disabilities.
fundraiser.donate_500_desc: Provides medical supplies for one patient for a month.
fundraiser.donate_1000_desc: Funds vocational training for one individual.
fundraiser.donate_5000_desc: Supports a family's livelihood for a month.
fundraiser.testimonial_1: "Donating to Anandwan feels like being part of a family that truly cares. I've seen the impact firsthand."
fundraiser.testimonial_2: "Every rupee counts here. I'm proud to support a cause that transforms lives."
fundraiser.be_change_desc: Your support can make a lasting difference in the lives of thousands.
